- National Sporting Club - Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Sporting_Club): # National Sporting Club. The **National Sporting Club** (**NSC**) was a private members' sporting club founded in London on 5 March 1891 by John Fleming and Arthur Frederick Bettinson. Operating from premises at 43 King Street, Covent Garden, it became the principal centre for elite professional boxing in Britain during the late nineteenth and early twentieth centuries.
